description_long: This object is a solar energy infrastructure installation featuring a rectangular array of solar panels mounted on a metallic frame. The panels are dark, likely made of photovoltaic cells, and are elevated above a base structure that appears to be concrete or metal, with red and gray markings. The mounting frame is composed of several support legs, giving the panels a slight tilt for optimal sunlight exposure. The base is elongated and flat, possibly serving as a foundation or housing for electrical components. No visible cabling or inverter is apparent from the exterior. The overall design suggests a ground-mounted solar array suitable for urban environments such as parking lots, sidewalks, or small public spaces.
description: A ground-mounted solar panel array with a tilted, dark photovoltaic surface on a metallic frame above a rectangular base with red and gray markings.
description_view_0: Side view showing the tilted solar panels and the elongated base with visible red and gray markings.
description_view_1: End-on view highlighting the narrow width of the array and the alignment of the panels above the base.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, again showing the tilted panels and the full length of the base structure.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the back of the solar panels and the supporting frame structure.
attribute_panel_orientation: tilted at approximately 20 degrees, orientation appears fixed
attribute_mounting_structure_type: ground-mounted with a metallic frame and support legs
attribute_array_configuration: single linear row of approximately 4 panels
